entry.title: SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Target Group

Anyone interested to know more about extending SAP S/4HANA Cloud using SAP Build

 

Background

What is Extensibility

When a customer has unique business requirements that are not fully catered by standard SAP S/4HANA then there is a need to extend the system. These extensions can be classified into 2 broad categories:

  • On-stack extensibility involves extending SAP S/4HANA directly within the core system using key user tools or ABAP.
  • Side-by-side extensibility uses SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) to develop custom applications or integrations outside the core S/4HANA system, ensuring greater flexibility and upgrade safety.

What is SAP Build

SAP Build ( including SAP Build Code) is the default extension development toolset on SAP BTP.

Build Tools under low-code spectrum:

  • SAP Build Apps - Create Mobile and Web applications
  • SAP Build Process Automation - Automate business processes and workflows
  • SAP Build Work Zone - Create Digital Workspaces and business sites

Build Tools under Pro-code spectrum:

SAP Build Code - Use Business Application Studio(BAS) and Cloud Application programming Model(CAP) to create Applications. And lot more.

What challenges are we trying to address here

  • Not easy to identify the available extension options for a given S/4HANA application directly in the system
  • Not easy to determine the right extension points (eg APIs, Events) for a given S/4HANA application
  • Not an integrated experience when extending SAP S/4HANA in a side-by-side manner on BTP

 

Introduction

  • A new Extensibility wizard is available with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 that provides both on-stack and side-by-side extension options consolidated into a single wizard.
  • It provides a guided experience to kick start extension creation and enables seamless navigation from SAP S/4HANA to SAP Build.
  • The wizard understands which S/4HANA application it has been started from and accordingly filters the extensions points( like API, Events) based on the business context.

This wizard can be accessed from any extensible SAP S/4HANA app as shown below. "Create Extension" option is available under the Me Area (User menu) on the top right.

On clicking the Create Extension option, the Extensibility Wizard opens up displaying the various extension options

Note: Setup steps mentioned next are needed as a prerequisite to be able to see the "Create Extension" option

 

Setup

Short summary of the setup steps:

  1. Subscribe to SAP Build Process Automation in your SAP BTP subaccount.
  2. Add your SAP S/4HANA cloud system in the BTP Global account under System landscape and register in a formation.
  3. Create 2 destinations in the BTP account pointing to UI service and API service of SAP S/4HANA. This is needed for communication from Build to SAP S/4HANA, for example to fetch business context, events and authorizations.
  4. Create a communication arrangement in SAP S/4HANA. This is to enable communication from SAP S/4HANA to SAP Build.
  5. Assign the required authorization in SAP S/4HANA to access the wizard.

Detailed setup steps:

  • BTP setup steps - link
  • S4 setup steps - link

 

Extension options in the Wizard ( Sep 2024 )

On-stack

Custom Field

Enables addition of new fields in desired business objects. The wizard opens the Custom Field App with the business contexts pre set.

 

Custom Logic

Enables addition of custom logic via Cloud BAdIs. Here as well the wizard opens the Custom Logic App with the business contexts pre set.

 

Side-by-Side

Custom Process

Create a Process in SAP Build Process Automation that starts based on an event from SAP S/4HANA. The business context of the starting SAP S/4HANA app is pre set in the wizard to easily identify the relevant events.

A detailed explanation of this process extension option is available in this blog from my colleague.

The extensibility wizard is expected to grow with more extension options in future.

 

Conclusion

The new SAP Build Extensibility wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides a simple guided experience to create extensions both on-stack and side-by-side.

If you have access to an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system, follow the setup steps and do explore the Extensibility Wizard !

entry.title: Creating side-by-side process extensions from SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
The release of SAP Build's new extensibility wizard forSAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release marks a significant step in streamlining the creation of on-stack extensions (within SAP S/4HANA Cloud) and side-by-side extensions (in SAP BTP) to help keep the core clean. The wizard, which can be accessed directly from any business application that is enabled for key user adaptation, allows key users and even developers to easily create custom process extensions in SAP Build Process Automation (SAP BTP) that can seamlessly integrate with SAP S/4HANA Cloud. In this blog, we will explore the use cases for process extensions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud and how the new wizard is a game-changer for customers looking to enhance their business processes

Side-by-side extensions

Side-by-side extensions run outside of SAP S/4HANA Cloud (or other business application) but interact with the business applications using standard extension points like APIs, events, etc. Side-by-side extensions can technically run on any application platform, nevertheless SAP provides and recommends its dedicated platform for extension: SAP BTP.

What are process extensions

Many applications in SAP S/4HANA use SAP Business Workflow enabling preconfigured workflow scenarios to be reused in various situations. The scenarios can either be implemented without any changes or configured for your business processes by making minor adjustments. On top of that, Flexible workflows allow you to define condition-based workflows for the pre-configured approval processes. For example, the flexible workflow for purchase requisitions allows you to define one-step or multi-step approval processes for purchase requisitions according to your requirements. These are pre-delivered as part of the SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud. However, your use case may go beyond a typical pre-delivered approval workflow which needs a side-by-side process extension. Here are some use case patterns which warrants a process extension in SAP BTP:

  • Processes that span across systems.
  • Processes that reach the users who are outside SAP S/4HANA.
  • Use certain capabilities from SAP BTP, like SAP AI Services.
  • Any other requirements that are not covered in the standard processes and workflows.

Example use case

Consider a specific use case where you want to make sure that every invoice created in the system has a reference to a purchase order without user intervention. There are multiple ways this can be achieved including a process extension. A side-by-side process extension in SAP Build Process Automation (in SAP BTP) can check if a purchase order is assigned for every new supplier invoice created in the system. If not, then the process shall identify the right purchase order using SAP AI Services in SAP BTP like Document Information Extraction, Data Attribute Recommendation, Invoice Object Recommendation, SAP AI Core, etc. When the machine learning model could not find the right match, then it could inform the procurement department member for further action. This process extension would run alongside the standard procurement process, adding the necessary functionality without altering the original process.

Why SAP Build Process Automation?

SAP Build Process Automation in SAP BTP with its rich development tools, integration capability and pre-built content is the recommended process extension platform for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud. SAP Build Process Automation has 500+ pre-built content packages for extending SAP and non-SAP applications. Most of those content is free-to-use and can be accessed from SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

With the SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition 2408 release, the extensibility wizard streamlines the creation of process extensions in SAP Build Process Automation. The process created in SAP Build Process Automation is deployed & executable in SAP BTP and can be triggered automatically when a business event is observed in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. 

Creating a process extension

Let us see how easy it is to create a process extension with the new wizard.

Pre-requisite:

  • Setup SAP Event Mesh or SAP Advanced Event Mesh

A business event-based triggering of process is a capability of SAP Build Process Automation. It requires SAP Event Mesh or SAP Advanced Event Mesh to observe the events in SAP S/4HANA Cloud to initiate a process in SAP Build Process Automation. Refer to this blog for the detailed setup instruction.

  • You have the right business role and can access the extensibility wizard as described in previous blog of this series.

Procedure

  • From any application which is enabled for key user adaptation, open the 'Me Area' to choose the new 'Create Extension' option to launch the extensibility wizard.

Choose Process Extension. (If you do not have entitlement to SAP Build Process Automation in the connected SAP BTP tenant, then this option will be disabled with a 'Not subscribed' ribbon in the tile).

Based on the application from where the extension was triggered, the wizard automatically identifies the available extension points (in 2408 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition, only business events are supported. More extension points is planned as future increments).

Choose Business Events, and proceed to next step.

The wizard also recognises the event objects that are relevant for the application and automatically applies a filter on the business object. Of course, you can override these filters and search any event that is needed for your use case. Select the appropriate event object and proceed to the next step.

All events of the selected event object are shown. Select the appropriate event and proceed to the next step.

Give a name and description for your extension project that will be created in SAP Build (SAP BTP). 

Confirm the changes in the Summary step or go back to edit any steps. This creates an extension project for based in the inputs given in the previous steps.

Once the project is created successfully, you can directly navigate to the project in SAP Build. 

When navigated into SAP Build (in SAP BTP), you can see a process automation project with a process created for you. 

When you open the process, you can see that the event selected in the wizard is automatically added. All the context from the selected business event is now available at your disposal. 

You can proceed to use the low-code process builder to create an extension process as needed. You can call any APIs like the SAP S/4HANA Cloud or SAP Cloud Integration models or any SAP BTP services, you can add a user task that creates a task in My Inbox or SAP Task Center, you can call an RPA bot automation, you can send notification emails, etc. Once the process is created, release a version and deploy the content into SAP BTP. 

When this process automation project is deployed in SAP BTP, it listens to the specific event in the connected SAP S/4HANA Cloud. When such an event is observed, it starts an instance of the process with the context that is available in the business event.

For more information on the lifecycle, refer to the help documentation of SAP Build Process Automation.

Key take aways

  • A brand-new wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is available with 2408 release to create on-stack and side-by-side extensions.
  • The wizard can be called from any application in SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition that is enabled for key user adaptation.
  • Discover the available process extension points based on the application that was used to start the wizard, through the concept of Business Context.
  • Create a side-by-side process extension in SAP Build Process Automation (SAP BTP) directly from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Look-ahead plans

  • Management of side-by-side extensions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.
  • More extension points for process extensions apart from the business event.
  • Enable the wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition.

entry.title: Build Process Automation Notifications Part 1 of 3 - Default Notifications
entry.content:
In September 2024, SAP Build Process Automation released standard notifications integrated by default into all running processes.
 
Now, with a simple configuration in BTP, all running tasks automatically generate notifications to the task recipient, keeping them up-to-date on their assignments wherever they are. Build Process Automation notifications use the SAP-standard Alert Notification Service, which means they are automatically sent over email, in-app notification center and mobile. More channels will be added in the future without any extra configuration work needed from administrators.
 
Here is how to get notifications:
  • enable the notifications feature in your BTP cockpit by following the adminstrator documentation here: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/enable-notifications 
  • add a task to a process
  • run the process

As you can see, there is no need to specify notification parameters as the default settings already handle the most common cases. In this scenario:
  • the notification will be sent by email and to the bell icon
    the bell icon is the notification center in SAP applications, like SAP Work Zone
  • there is no need to specify recipients twice
    the recipients will be the recipients of the task
  • each user decides if they want mobile notifications
    the recipients of the notification can personalize, to turn off channels like email or notification center, or to turn on notifications for mobile
SAP Build Process Automation processes control finely-tuned sequences of tasks, decisions and applications in order to ensure that company workflows run predictably and error-free. When employees are required to contribute, notifications help get their attention precisely at the moment it is needed.
 
The notifications provide context and quickly let the recipient understand and decide what needs to get done. Opening the tasks in MyInbox or the modern SAP Task Center gives more details and actions. In a soon-to-be-released upgrade, links will point directly to the tasks and the personalization views.

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation – SMTP Mail Destination using Microsoft 365 email provider
entry.content:
A common use case for SAP Build Process Automation is sending emails to end users when workflow tasks are assigned, or triggering emails to the workflow initiator when a task is approved or rejected. To use Email step in your process, you need to have an active mail server configured, and a destination must be set up in the SAP BTP subaccount where the Build Process Automation subscription is available.

SAP Help documentation to create SMTP mail destination in SAP BTP can be found here: Configuring SMTP Mail Destination.

There are various SMTP server options available for handling outgoing emails. A common customer question is how to configure this with Microsoft and what steps are involved.

Note: Microsoft 365 no longer allows connections to Outlook 365 via Basic Authentication. As a best practice, it's recommended to use more advanced protocols like OAuth2 for enhanced security.

More information - https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/exchange/client-developer/legacy-protocols/how-to-authenticate-an-imap-pop-smtp-application-by-using-oauth

In this blog post, I will guide you through the process of setting up an SMTP server with Microsoft and integrating it with SAP Build Process Automation using OAuth2Password.

The configurations on the Microsoft side must be carried out by your organization’s Microsoft Administrators.

Pre-requisites

  1. A tenant in Microsoft Azure Entra ID(Active Directory).
  2. A user who has an active Outlook 365 subscription in the same tenant.

Configurations to be completed in Microsoft Azure

Step 1: Use your Admin account to sign in to your Azure tenant at https://portal.azure.com. Upon logging in, you will be taken to the Microsoft Azure Home Page, as shown below.

Step 2: In the search bar at the top, type "Microsoft Entra ID" and select it from the list of results to proceed.

Step 3: Navigate to Manage and then select App Registrations.

Step 4: Select New Registration.

Step 5: Provide a suitable name for your application and select "Accounts in any organizational directory (Any Microsoft Entra ID tenant - Multitenant)." You can then proceed to register your application.

The app is now registered in Microsoft Entra ID and is displayed as shown below. Make sure to save the Application (client) ID and Directory (tenant) ID, as they will be needed later.

Step 6: Under Manage, navigate to Certificates & Secrets.

Save the Application (client) ID, which we will refer to as <Application (client) ID>. This will be used during the configuration of the Destination in SAP BTP.

Save the Directory (tenant) ID, which we will refer to as <Directory (tenant) ID>, and it will be used when configuring the Destination in SAP BTP.

Step 7: Generate a new client secret.

Enter a description and set the expiry date according to your organization’s policy. If there is an end date, ensure you generate a new secret before the old one expires and update any references to the old secret.

Save both the Secret ID and Value for future use, although only the Value will be needed. We will refer Value as <Secret Value> which will be used while configuring Destination in SAP BTP.

Step 8: Next, navigate to API Permissions.

Select "Add a permission".

Select “Microsoft Graph”.

Choose Delegated permissions.

Under Select permissions, search for SMTP.Send and choose the permission as shown below.

The permissions are displayed as shown below. You can either keep the User.Read permission or remove it. Additionally, you will need to grant admin consent for the user.

Step 9: As we are using OAuth2Password, Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) needs to be disabled.

Note: This will not be required once Authentication Type OAuth2ClientCredentials is supported. 

In the Microsoft Entra Admin Center, go to Identity, then Protection, and select Conditional Access.

Go to Policy Snapshot

Snapshots below

Choose Multifactor Authentication for Microsoft partners and vendors, then under the Enable Policy section, switch the toggle to Off and click Save.

Repeat the same process for all the available default policies.

Note: Your organization may have additional custom policies that require special attention. Ensure that you follow your organizational policies, as they may differ from the default settings.

Step 10: Navigate to the Exchange Admin Center at https://admin.exchange.microsoft.com, go to Settings, then MailFlow, and uncheck the option for “Turn Off SMTP Auth protocol for your organization.”

Step 11: Go to https://admin.microsoft.com, then navigate to Users > Active users, and select the user that will be configured for outgoing emails.

Ensure that "Authenticated SMTP" is enabled.

Configurations to be completed in SAP BTP

Create a Destination with name sap_process_automation_mail in the BTP subaccount where SAP Build Process Automation is subscribed.

Name

sap_process_automation_mail

Type

MAIL

Description

Destination to Microsoft SMTP Mail Serer

Proxy Type

Internet

Authentication

OAuth2Password

User

<Email ID of the User>

Password

<Password of the User>

Client ID

<Application (client) ID>

Client Secret

<Secret Value>

Token Service URL

https://login.microsoftonline.com/<Directory (tenant) ID>/oauth2/v2.0/token

Note: <Application (client) ID>, <Secret Value> and <Directory (tenant) ID> is from steps 6 and 7.

Additional Properties

mail.smtp.auth

true

mail.smtp.auth.mechanisms

XOAUTH2

mail.smtp.from

<User from which you want to send Email> - Can be any Email ID

mail.smtp.host

smtp.office365.com

mail.smtp.port

587

mail.smtp.ssl.enable

false

mail.smtp.starttls.enable

true

mail.smtp.starttls.required

true

mail.transport.protocol

smtp

scope

https://outlook.office.com/SMTP.send

To perform the test, log in to SAP Build Process Automation and navigate to Control Tower > Mail Server.

Note: If the above screen is not visible and you have correctly configured everything in BTP destinations, the sign-in to Microsoft Entra ID likely failed. Refer to the Troubleshooting section below for guidance on checking errors.

Send a Test Email

Enter the Email ID to which you would like to Send a Test Email.

 

Troubleshooting

There are couple of places where you can troubleshoot and find what is going wrong.

  1. SAP BTP subaccount Destination Logs – This can be challenging as end users do not have direct access to Destination Logs. A support ticket to SAP must be raised to get the logs.
  2. Microsoft Azure Entra ID Tenant – This can be found under Monitoring > Sign-in Logs.

Filter using the Application Name created for Microsoft Entra ID Tenant.

entry.title: SAP Developer News, September 19th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/09/19/sap-developer-news-september-19th-2024/

DESCRIPTION

Devtoberfest Launch

  • Devtoberfest Community Home: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/gh-p/Devtoberfest
  • Devtoberfest Events Calendar: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/eb-p/devtoberfest-events?calendar=true
  • Devtoberfest Gameboard: https://devrel-tools-prod-scn-badges-srv.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/devtoberfestContest/scnId.Here
  • Devtoberfest Playlist on YouTube: https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQDHz97qsNTNAE2jnUKj8X5d&si=R26dQIns-1e97fU0

SAP Cloud Application Programming Model August 2024 Release

  • Release notes https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/releases/aug24

Devtoberfest Crossword Puzzle

  • https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/devtoberfest2024-sap-crossword.html

Transporting content in SAP Build Apps

  •  https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-builders-blog-posts/transporting-content-in-sap-build-apps-sap-cloud-transport-management/ba-p/13737478

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro
0:10 Devtoberfest Launch
2:09 SAP Cloud Application Programming Model August 2024 Release
7:56 Devtoberfest Puzzle
9:06 Transporting content in SAP Build Apps

Transcript

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for September 19th, 2024.

[Sheena] Hello SAP community. The much-awaited Detoberfest 2024 is here, running from September 23rd to October 25th. Devtoberfest helps to prepare for SAP TechEd and offers a mix of educational sessions, hands-on developer tools, and fun activities like Petoberfest, Bugtoberfest, and Spooktoberfest. It is a point-based contest, and the Devtoberfest animated contest game board is already live. You can earn points by attending sessions, completing developer tutorials linked to weekly topics or engaging in activities that benefit the SAP Developer Community.

The grand prize is a retreat at an SAP TechEd Roadshow stop in 2025. Exciting, right? To start participating, join the Devtoberfest group after reviewing the legal terms and conditions posted there.

We will kickstart Devtoberfest on Monday, September 23rd, which is the ABAP/CAP Day. Week 1, we are bringing to you many exciting sessions in ABAP, mainly on clean core extensibility, building partner products on SAP BTP, major updates in embedded analytics, and data integration in ABAP Cloud with SQL services and CDS external entities. Week 1 also covers an exciting session on CAP, it's time to TypeScript (CAP edition).

On tooling Tuesdays, we'll focus on SAP Build Process Automation, Build Code and Build Apps.

Integration Wednesdays will have sessions on FlashPy, CPI Helper, and CPI Lint.

MAD Thursdays will focus on machine learning, AI, and data with sessions on SAP Generative AI Hub, SAP Datasphere, etc.

Frontend Fridays will have sessions on UI5 Freestyle and SAP Fiori tools.

Don't forget to participate in the fun Friday event Petoberfest by posting pictures with your pets at your workstation. So join us in this fun ride of learning and enjoy. Good morning.

[DJ] The latest release of CAP August of 2024, in fact, is out. And with it, there are improvements and fixes to the latest major release 8, which came out earlier this year, in the form of CAP Node.js 8.2.1 and CAP Java 3.2.0. Let me take this opportunity on my walk this morning to go through some of those highlights.

So first up, when the latest major release of CAP came out earlier this year, the new database services went GA, including the database service for SAP HANA. One of the benefits of this new database service for the Node.js runtime is that HANA native associations were no longer required to represent associations at the CDS level. This was great, mostly because it brought longer deploy times when we used SAP HANA Native Associations, plus there was the chance of indirect dependencies also being created. And if you're wondering about the Java runtime, the Java runtime hasn't used native SAP HANA Associations for a while. Anyway, at the time, the release notes advised us to switch off the HANA native associations using configuration. With this August 2024 release, that switch is now automatic. When you start a new project and do, for example, CDS add HANA, you get a switch in the configuration that turns these native associations off by default.

In the area of the CDS language and compiler, you can now use CDS deploy dash dash script to generate a SQL script as a starting point or manual schema migration. Note that in contrast to the existing dash dash dry option, it accepts and produces DDL statements that could lead to data loss. So are A, not supported for automatic schema migration and B, contains relevant warnings in the right places.

In the Node.js runtime, there are also some important changes. One of which is that linked definitions, the class that is used to represent internally structures of services and more, now no longer have the convenience methods such as for each map filter and so on that you would normally expect to have to process the linked definitions as arrays. Because basically linked definitions are not arrays, they're more than that. So these methods have been removed. In my opinion, this is great because it reminds us that we have to treat the data as it is. And so one thing to do is maybe convert the data to arrays using the rest parameter inside, for example, an array, literal array constructor.

Another improvement is that you can now programmatically, i.e. dynamically at runtime, control and activate the CDS debug output at runtime. This means you can turn on debug for cat modules as you go.

In the Java runtime, there's now beta level support for recursive hierarchies with Odata v4 in SAP HANA. Recursive hierarchy is an experimental term in the OData hierarchy vocabulary and is used, for example, with SAP Fiori tree tables. Treetables. You can also now define the name of the Java interfaces generated for CDS entities and structured types using a new annotation. In the multi-tenancy area, activating extensions using the SAP extensibility service can now be done asynchronously. You achieve this by sending a prefer header in the HTTP request with the value of respond async. This in my opinion is a really neat way of coordinating this using the RFC 7240 standard authored by an old acquaintance of mine.

Well I'm almost home but there's just time to tell you about improvements in the tools area. So first for all you IntelliJ users out there the SAP CDS language support plugin is now available on the JetBrains marketplace which means it's directly installable now. Great! For all you TypeScript users there's a new CLI command "CDS TSX", which in contrast to the existing CDS TS command uses a different faster engine. This feature is in beta still at the moment.

So I'm back home. There's one more thing to tell you about and that is if you want to use the SAP Cloud Logging Service instead of the SAP Application Logging Service, you can do that. And there's an option to use telemetry as well, including an option to use Dynatrace. What's not to like? Anyway, that's it for this August 2024 release. See See you next time.

[Shrini and Daniel] Hello, everybody. I'm at Tbilisi, Georgia with Daniel at the EPUB office doing SAP Build CodeJam. It's really great to be here. Yeah, it's been a lot of fun and because it's only a couple days before Devtoberfest, we wanted to tell you something about the contest. So the contest you can earn enough points to get a trip to TechEd perhaps next year and one of the ways to get points is by doing the traditional SAP crossword. So there are topics about SAP Build and JavaScript and even a reference to Analytics Cloud and it would help to know something about Sabrina Carpenter. Can anybody do the Cross Word? Yes, definitely. Of course, it helps to know a little bit about SAP. There are 33 questions. Fairly easy, a few tougher ones, but I think everybody can do it, and it might even be worth a couple thousand points. And it's already published, so even before the Devtoberfest starts, you can accumulate points. So that's pretty good. That's good to know. Yeah. Good luck to everybody.

[Rekha] Hello, everybody. We all know the importance of having development, test, and productive environments during the application development. SAP has recently introduced project versioning for SAP Build apps, web, and mobile projects. This allows you to transport the released project versions between development, testing, and productive subaccounts on SAP BTP. A transport setup of Cloud Transport Management Service, CTMS, Content Agent Service, CAS, and BTP destinations are required to start transporting SAP Build Apps projects between the three tiered dev test prod landscapes. There are four types of subaccounts or tenants involved in the scenario. A source subaccount, this is where the actual development takes place. A target, under this we have two subaccounts, testing, and production, and a central sub-account to administer the content transport between the source and target sub-accounts. In order to enable the transport, you will have to enable the Cloud Foundry, subscribe to SAP Build apps and content agent services, create an instance of content agent, and generate a service key on the source and target sub-accounts.

On the central sub-account, you need to subscribe to Cloud Transport Management service, create an instance of Cloud Transport Management, and generate a service key. The MBS role collection has to be enabled in BTP cockpit to authorize the users for performing the transport activities. SAP Burn Lobby configuration has to be done in the control tower in order to set the sub-account type to decide if it's a dev, test, or prod subaccount. Then BTP destinations has to be set from source to central subaccount, and then from central to target, that is the test and prod subaccounts. Finally, set up the transport landscape using the Cloud Transport Management user interface. For more details on this topic, do refer to this interesting blog post by SAP Build expert Mari Heary. Link is provided in the description box. Enjoy learning and thank you.

entry.title: Devtoberfest 2024 - Tooling (SAP Build) Sessions
entry.content:
Today is the first day of Devoberfest!!

Make sure you partake of the:

  • Contest -- grand prize is a trip to an SAP TechEd event with a lot of extras; and even though it's several years' old I still love the gameboard
  • Petoberfest – post a picture of your pet 🐢
  • Sessions – find all the sessions in the event calendar 
  • 👉 The annual SAP Crossword Puzzle – if you have the skill, you can earn 1800 points!

Devtoberfest 2024 sessions

Here's a quick rundown of the SAP Build "Tooling Tuesday" sessions, which touch on the following topics:

  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Build Work Zone
  • SAP Build Code
  • MDK
  • S/4HANA + extensions
  • Integration Suite, including event mesh
  • CAP
  • AI Core
  • GenAI + Joule
  • SAP Cloud Transport Management

That's just one day of Devtoberfest!! In each session, you'll have a chance to ask questions directly to the presenter, who will answer them live or in the chat.

September 24

  • 🟣 Transform Data with Scripting in SAP Build Process Automation

  • 🟣 Develop a Full-Stack CAP Application with SAP Build Code

  • 🟣 Develop with SAP Build Apps Like a Pro

  • 🟣 Real-World S/4HANA Invoice Approval Process with SAP Build Process Automation

October 1

  • 🟣 SAP Build Apps 2049: Soon with UI5 and GenAI?

  • 🟣 Enable Work Zone to Consume Business Solutions from Other BTP Subaccounts (content federation)

  • 🟣 Cross-Platform Mobile App Development with SAP Build Code and the MDK

  • 🟣 Putting It All Together: S/4HANA Retail Pricing App Using SAP Build, AI, Event Mesh and More

October 15

  • 🟣 S/4HANA Extensions: Start Fast with the New Extensibility Wizard

  • 🟣 Guided Experiences with SAP Build Process Automation and Work Zone

  • 🟣 Develop Even Faster (!) with SAP Build Apps Templates

  • 🟣 Boost Development with GenAI in SAP Build Process Automation

October 22

  • 🟣 Chatbot Development Made Easy with SAP AI Core and Build Apps

  • 🟣 Set Up Rules for Who Can Create and Transport SAP Build Projects

  • 🟣 Leveraging Decisions and Business Rules in SAP Build Process Automation

  • 🟣 Extend Joule with Your Own Skills

Learn a lot about SAP development tools, have a lot of fun – and maybe we'll see you at an SAP TechEd event next year 😸

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Build – Q3 2024 Edition
entry.content:
Greetings, Builders! We’re pleased to bring you the Q3 Edition of our What’s New in SAP Build series. In this edition, we’re covering product-specific updates for:

  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Start, SAP Task Center
  • SAP Build Code

SAP Build Apps

Release management and project lifecycle management: It’s now possible to release and transport projects across different tenants using SAP Cloud Transport Management System. You can transport project versions between subaccounts while isolating development, testing, and productive deployment of SAP Build Apps applications to their own landscapes. To get started, see this blog post.

Performant table list with large dataset from S/4HANA: Managing and displaying large amounts of data in an application can be challenging and cause performance issues. Check out this example of creating a performant list page for an S/4HANA Cloud endpoint with 65k+ records - see the blog post.

Copying pages within one project: This feature enables users to copy and duplicate a page within a project in SAP Build Apps – a simple, yet powerful, add-on to further speed up development.

New Build Service: We recently launched a new and improved experience for deploying application packages. The build service includes managing image assets, iOS usage strings, mobile service enablement, package creation, and configuration management. The updated UI provides a much smoother experience for developers at the deployment phase, ensuring that every requirement is met and that your apps are ready to publish. Try it out today!

  • Also check out Ludo Noens’ series on Consuming SAP with SAP Build Apps – Creating a CRUD Application
  • Learn more about what’s coming up on the SAP Build Apps Roadmap.

SAP Build Process Automation

Script Task Editor: It's now possible to write JavaScript code in the new Script Editor, allowing the execution of complex data type transformations and additional operations on the process context. Previously this was limited to automation - now it includes process inputs and custom variables, facilitating advanced data manipulation and transformation. See more in the blog post.

Scheduled trigger: Now you can schedule process automations based on user-defined time intervals and dates at runtime. This allows you to enable system-centric and time-based process automation use cases, such as working and nonworking days in creating your automation scenarios to avoid resource mismatches.

Additional Outcomes in Forms and UI5 Support for Tasks: This enables citizen developers to configure additional outcome actions in the process to let approvers request rework in case of missing or wrong information in approval forms. You can also start filling out multiple forms efficiently through a guided experience.

Advanced Event Project Creation with Manual Specification Upload: This allows users to upload business event specifications for the creation of an event project in the lobby. The new feature adds support for business events across lines of business (LOBs) and custom events, including third-party events that are supported at runtime using SAP Event Mesh.

Process Visibility enhancements: These include actionable insights, instance tracking for requestors and additional monitoring capabilities via CALM. Now you can turn insights into meaningful outcomes and enable the triggering of escalation flows based on visibility insights, to directly react and resolve critical situations. This enables auditable and governed escalation resolution, instead of having disconnected communication through different channels.

More on SAP Build Process Automation:

  • See the What's New Viewer for more updates, and improvements.
  • See SAP Road Map Explorer for more upcoming features and focus areas.

SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Start, and SAP Task Center

SAP Build Work Zone, both editions

HTML5 Business Solution as a Content Provider: With the latest update in SAP Build Work Zone, you can now expose BTP-based applications as Work Zone content, like roles, spaces, and pages, in the 'Common Data Model' (CDM) format. As a result, you can easily configure and enable access to BTP applications from a Work Zone instance, even across subaccounts. See more in the Help Portal and this blog post.

SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition

Define Sort Order of pages and spaces: Administrators can now configure how spaces and pages are sorted on the menu bar of SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition. This feature is only applicable if you're working in the new experience view mode for spaces and pages. See more in the Help Portal.

Import and Export of Pages and Spaces: Content manager tools will be enhanced to enable administrators to export and import custom (locally created) spaces and pages between landscapes.

SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition

Integrated guided experience across SAP Build Work Zone and Process Automation*: Guided processes are now available as an artifact in SAP Build Process Automation. In addition to the previously available “no template” wizards, it is now possible to create guided, process-based wizards in SAP Build Work Zone.

*Note: This will become GA in October 2024. 

SAP Start

Directly navigate to product home pages of integrated cloud solutions: The SAP Start site offers a new product section to easily see and navigate to the product home pages of the integrated solutions. This enables transparency of available cloud solutions for the current user, and quick and easy navigation to launch the product home page of the desired solution.

SAP Task Center

ITCE partner page: SAP Task Center now allows connections with third-party task providers. The first solution to leverage this capability is TaskConnect, developed by ITCE in collaboration with SAP architects and technical consultants. The application will soon be available on the SAP Store as well, offering a comprehensive configuration guide, installation manuals and BPMN diagrams. For more information about TaskConnect, see here.

More on SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Start and SAP Task Center:

  • Check out the roadmaps for SAP Build Work Zone standard & advanced edition and SAP Start / SAP Task Center
  • What's New Viewers: Standard edition / Advanced edition / SAP Start / SAP Task Center 

SAP Build Code

New AI Features: In SAP Build Code, you can now use the Joule copilot, our generative AI assistant, to write code by providing natural language prompts. For a detailed explanation of Joule, please read this article. In the last months we added many new features to this capability including the following highlights:

  • Support SAP S/4HANA Cloud Service Integration for Data Model and App Logic: It is now possible to use Joule to generate code for your integrated SAP S/4HANA Cloud services.
  • Major UX Update of Joule in SAP Build Code: With the latest UX update of Joule within SAP Build Code we enhanced the usability, streamlined workflows, and created a more intuitive interface, boosting productivity and satisfaction.
  • Generative AI support for productivity tools: We now allow you to use Joule directly from the storyboard.
  • Use Joule to enhance your already existing data models and services: It is now possible to modify already existing data model and service entities using a prompt.

Why SAP Build Code: Check out this blog post by Product Manager Rupa Maity describing why to move to SAP Build Code.

Stay updated on the latest Build Code innovations: Do you want to stay informed about the latest product updates for SAP Build Code? Follow our tag on SAP Community and subscribe to our SAP Build Code Newsletter. 

  • See the latest update product updates from September 2024 for SAP Build Code.
  • Learn more on the SAP Build Code Community.

--- 

💡Please note this blog post provides a highlight of the latest features and enhancements. 

  • For comprehensive list of all updates, please refer to our Help Portal What’s New in SAP Build.
  • Join the SAP Builders group for product resources and community questions.
  • Devtoberfest is here! 🥨 Don't miss our SAP Build-themed sessions and more.
  • ...and that means SAP TechEd is right around the corner! Explore SAP Build at TechEd 2024.

Which feature are you most excited to test out? Let us know in the comments!

entry.title: SAP Developer News, September 26th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast Version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/09/26/sap-developer-news-september-26th-2024/

DESCRIPTION

Extensibility Wizard

  • Blog by Karthik Thirunavukkarasan, overview of Extensibility Wizard: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-build-extensibility-wizard-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/ba-p/13867427
  • Blog by Seshadri Sreenivas Ramanarayanan, on side-by-side extensions with SAP Build process Automation: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/creating-side-by-side-process-extensions-from-sap-s-4hana-cloud/ba-p/13867657
  • Documentation:
    • Setup on BTP: https://help.sap.com/docs/build/sap-build-core/use-extensions?version=Cloud
    • Setup on S/4HANA Cloud: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/b2ee5784ccfc45d5acf42d4c60eb5270.html
  • Antonio Maradiaga’s videos on setting up event mesh for SAP Build Process Automation:
    • SAP Tech Bytes: Configuring SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh
    • SAP Tech Bytes: Delivering events from SAP S/4HANA Cloud to SAP Build Process Automation via AEM
  • Devtoberfest Session: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/s-4hana-extensions-start-fast-with-the-new-extensibility-wizard/ev-p/13795541
  • Petoberfest: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest-discussions/petoberfest-2024/m-p/13872559

Devtoberfest Week 1 Recap

  • Devtoberfest Homepage: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/gh-p/Devtoberfest
  • Devtoberfest Contest Week 1: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest-blog-posts/devtoberfest-2024-week-1-contest-activities/ba-p/13872705
  • Devtoberfest Event Calendar: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/eb-p/devtoberfest-events?calendar=true
  • Devtoberfest Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQDHz97qsNTNAE2jnUKj8X5d

Devtoberfest Week 2 Preview

  • Devtoberfest Contest Week 2: Coming Soon
  • Devtoberfest Event Calendar: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/eb-p/devtoberfest-events?calendar=true
  • Devtoberfest Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQDHz97qsNTNAE2jnUKj8X5d

SAP TechEd Community Organized Watch Party

  • SAP TechEd: https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html
  • SAP Community Welcome: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog/session/1723237330354001PDnG
  • SAP Community Watch Zurich: https://community.sap.com/t5/z%C3%BCrich-events/sap-teched-virtual-your-sap-community-viewing-party-zurich/ec-p/13873047#M1
  • SAP Community Watch Hamburg: https://community.sap.com/t5/hamburg-events/sap-teched-virtual-your-sap-community-viewing-party-hamburg/ev-p/13875609
  • SAP Community Watch Sao Paulo: https://community.sap.com/t5/s%C3%A3o-paulo-events/sap-teched-virtual-your-sap-community-viewing-party-s%C3%A3o-paulo/ec-p/13875927#M3
  • SAP Inside Track Bengaluru: https://community.sap.com/t5/bengaluru-events/announcing-sap-inside-track-bengaluru-5th-october-2024/ev-p/13855659

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro
0:10 Extensibility Wizard
2:09 Devtoberfest Week 1 Recap
4:03 Devtoberfest Week 2 Preview
6:56 SAP TechEd Community Watch Party

Transcript

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for September 26, 2024.

[Daniel and Pepper] For Petoberfest, I'm here with Pepper, and we're here to talk about the Extensibility Wizard, which was released last week and let users create extensions right from within the UI of their SAP app. For the first release, it will work in S/4HANA Cloud and will let you create custom logic, custom fields known as on stack extensions or custom or extension processes known as side-by-side extensions. [Don't go to sleep.]

One of the big benefits is that it's context aware. So for example, if I'm in the sales order application, it will offer me sales order related event triggers to go with my process. There's a little bit of setup involved. So your S/4 HANA Cloud has to know about your SAP BTP and SAP Build installations. That's fairly simple, just involves destinations, trust and communication arrangements. You also have to set up Event Mesh so that your events from S/4 HANA can arrive to process automation.

Luckily, my esteemed colleague Antonio Larediaga has already created two videos to show you very simply how to set this up. One to get your events from S/4 HANA into Event Mesh and then from Event Mesh to Process Automation. The extensibility wizard will undoubtedly be the talk of TechEd and that's why we've set up a Devtoberfest session where Sesh and Kartik can talk at length about how you set this up and how How do you use it? And you will be able to ask them questions. I will put links to all of the videos, the blogs, and the Devtoberfest session in the description.

[Say goodbye.] Bye-bye. 🐶

[Thomas] It seems like just the other day we were starting our planning for this year's Devtoberfest. And here we are, nearly at the end of the first week of the four-week event. My how's time flown. Our team has been super busy both preparing for SAP TechEd virtual, TechEd on tour, as well as delivering these great Devtoberfest sessions to you. At the end of the first week, we've had a really packed week, great attendance. Up over last year, some more people are finding their way to Devtoberfest, which makes us very happy. We've had some excellent sessions across all the topic days.

Keep in mind that Monday is ABAP and CAP day. Tuesday is our day for tooling, Tooling Tuesday, where we're looking at SAP Build, Build Apps, Build Code, Build Process Automation, and how all these tools are bringing innovations for CAP development, low-code, no-code development, and ABAP development as well. Wednesday is our Integration Day. Thursday is MAD, our Machine Learning, AI, and Data Day. And then finally, Friday is both our Front-end Friday, which looks at SAP UI5 and Fiori and other topics related to front-end tools. But Friday is also our Fun Friday, where we have special activities.

Depending upon when you're watching this news item, There may still be time to enter into Petoberfest. Uh, that is our Fun Friday activity for September 27th. Post a picture of your pet in your work environment and, and you can earn some bonus points as well as have fun checking out everybody else's pet pictures as well.

Next Friday, we're going to have another special event. It's going to be our Techtoberfest day on, uh, October the 4th. And on that day, we're going to be asking you to post pictures of your work environment. Show us your laptop set up, your desk set up, whatever cool kind of thing you have set up in your work environment.

Hey, in week two, we will repeat the same daily track structure that you've that you've experienced in this first week. In fact, all four weeks will follow the same track structure, but our Monday is particularly packed with eight individual sessions across CAP and ABAP. We've got some really great things coming in that day. And it's going to be chock full of content. On Tuesday, I'm particularly interested to see the Build Apps, what's coming soon, where they're going to show both new AI innovations and the introduction of SAP UI5 output for Build Apps. Something that I've personally been looking for, for a while. We've got some cool stuff on the integration day around the event architecture and cloud events. We have content on the mad day around generative AI and using CAP with generative AI as well. So a real generative AI kind of day for that particular Thursday. And then finally, for our front end day, we have building elegant applications with Fiori for and Build Code for Android and an iOS Building applications for iOS as well. So we really have a mobile day focused on both Android and iOS

So we've got quite a selection of content for you all throughout the week and keep in mind once we finish week two we'll go directly into TechEd Virtual brought to you live from Germany And then we'll come back with more Devtoberfest, two more weeks of Devtoberfest after TechEd Virtual. And don't forget to enter into the contest. We have activities every day of the week, either by filling out validation tutorials for what you've just saw in your lecture sessions or completing tutorials that go along with them as well. So we've got lots of fun ways for you to earn points towards the big prize that you could win, win, which is, of course, a trip to one of the TechEd in-person events next year. And keep an eye out for TechEd on Tour, where from the ASUG TechConnect event, we're going to be bringing the winners from last year's Devtoberfest. We'll have some great pictures and videos from that event when it takes place in early November. So we look forward to seeing you in all the Devtoberfest and TechEd activities that are coming your way this month.

[Josh] Hey everybody. Are you as excited as I am that it is almost time for SAP TechEd? That's right. October 8th and 9th, SAP TechEd is happening this year. We're going to be coming to you live from Heidelberg, Germany. But if you can't be with us because it's virtual and you still want to talk with others and meet and watch the entire event, you can organize your own watch party. I know that several watch parties have already been organized for the SAP TechEd keynote, which is an hour and a half. It's going to be Michael Amling, Mohammed Alam, several others are going to be putting lots of great information out in this year's keynote. And right after that, for 30 minutes, me and Nora are going to get together and we're going to be hosting a community welcome.

So lots of watch parties are being organized around the world. The SAP community has several websites that I'll put links to in this video so you can see where others are getting together and maybe you can organize your own watch party as well. You can set it up, you can organize it on the SAP community website, and just let others know that you're going to be getting together to watch the Keynote and the Community Welcome and any other parts of SAP TechEd that you want to watch with others. I'll put a link to the one in Zurich, Hamburg, Sao Paulo, and then I think there's going to be one coming from Bengaluru, there's an SAP Inside Track, an SIT event that is happening on October 5th, and if you register for that, I believe you get an invite to the watch party, which is again happening on October 8th, when SAP's TechEd first day is going to go live at 9 a.m. Central European Time. I don't know where you are, so you have to figure out your own times on yourself. That's it, and I can't wait to see you from TechEd.

entry.title: Asset Revaluation & Acquisition in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition via SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
This blog is intended to complement my previous blogs on automation and augment Finance Asset Accounting scenario with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition as described with available scope items for Asset Revaluation (J62) and SAP Help documentation

A Professional Services company wanted to streamline its operations, particularly in managing the intricate logistics of end-to-end fixed asset acquisition and posting asset revaluation processes. 

Company discovers asset revaluation with seamless automation. By effortlessly collecting data from Asset Accountants through various document formats, including PDFs, we streamline the entire process.

In comprehensive approach, company identify assets acquired both in the current fiscal year and previous fiscal years. Each revaluation includes meticulous details such as Ledger information, Fixed Asset Revaluation Acquisition Amounts, Current Year Amounts, and the increments by which the original acquisition costs (APC) are adjusted.  In addition, specific details around corresponding cumulative value for ordinary revaluation are included.

SAP Build Process Automation's low-code solutions, designed to empower users to create and manage processes and workflows effortlessly. With the no-code process builder, enhancing enterprise applications and business sites is as simple as drag-and-drop.

 

Automation for asset accounting is driven by SAP Build Process Automation Actions (API), and rapidly developed UI-based automations for both SAP and third-party applications using the built-in automation recorder.

Decision management capabilities transform corporate policies and business rules into repeatable processes. The intuitive business rules editor lets users create decision tables, similar to spreadsheets, or text-based rules.

To streamline access and enhance operations, SAP Build Process Automation offer a unified launchpad and task center, providing a seamless user experience. This allows business users to easily access and manage notifications and tasks from various solutions, whether via web or mobile.

In a custom scenario evaluated by the Professional Services company, SAP Build Process Automation demonstrates how it can simplify process and task automation for business users without any coding required. The platform's built-in AI capabilities facilitate intelligent document processing, effortlessly extracting data from both structured and unstructured documents, and seamlessly transferring it to your enterprise systems.

In specific customer business use case, SAP Build Process Automation enhance accuracy by using document templates instead of relying solely on pre-trained models. This is achieved by uploading the business document to the service and creating a document template artifact, which can extract data from various file types (e.g., PDFs) as required for the specific business case.

Company evaluates the SAP Build Process Automation to elevate the efficiency and precision of their Asset management business operations, providing a robust solution for intelligent document processing and seamless integration with enterprise systems.

Enterprise Automation with SAP serves as the foundational layer for integrating Generative AI into your business processes. By embedding AI models into orchestrated workflows and task automations, you can significantly enhance the level of automation and efficiency in your operations. You can learn more with roadmap items

In future enhancements, customer plan to explore the benefits of AI-powered artifact creation and recommendations, areas not covered in the current scope.

  • Generative AI will streamline the creation of process and rules artifacts, leading to more efficient process analysis, automation, and integration capabilities.
  • AI-powered tools will generate integration flows that support seamless scaling and address deviations through advanced anomaly detection.

You can stay ahead of the curve by leveraging the latest AI advancements to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of your business operations.

Furthermore, Event-Driven Architectures with SAP Build Process Automation empower business users to fully automate processes in response to business events. (see Business Accelerator Hub for more details).  You can utilize existing business event objects or design custom ones to monitor and subscribe to specific occurrences within your system of records, proactively triggering automations.

SAP Build Process Automation seamlessly integrates Document Information Extraction capabilities, effortlessly extracting relevant data from both structured and unstructured documents, such as scans.

Key data points, including asset acquisition date, useful life, business transaction type, and debit/credit indicators, are captured to facilitate year-end closing and the display of transactions in the asset history sheet.  It adeptly recognizes whether a revaluation is an increase or a reduction, and accurately extracts Revaluation Amounts for both APC (Acquisition Costs) and Ordinary Depreciation.

Leveraging AI-based document processing, SAP Build Process Automation enhances document extraction with features like automatic template detection and prebuilt activities for efficient data extraction. The system also can support data enrichment, like linking asset and company information from templates to match codes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, and can automatically align text.

This AI-driven approach to document information extraction and recommendations underscores customer’s commitment to improving their end-user experience and maintaining a competitive edge in the market.

Consider a typical business scenario where automating asset revaluation and verifying asset posted transactions in specific ledger journal entries is essential for an Asset Accountant.

Using SAP Build Process Automation's tasks, it becomes reality to leverage API “Fixed Asset – Post Asset Revaluation” (API_FIXEDASSETREVALUATION_G4BA) to streamline the revaluation process for multiple assets in a single request. This not only enhances data accuracy but also reduces costs compared to manual revaluation entries.

API “Fixed Asset – Post Asset Revaluation”

The automation action task accesses the API, incorporating nearly all fields from the Fiori app “Fixed Asset – Post Asset Revaluation” (F7775), allowing Asset Accountants to re-valuate assets for selected ledgers and depreciation areas. Revaluations can be applied to acquisition and production costs (APCs) and ordinary depreciation amounts.

To re-valuate an asset, specify the revaluation amount by which the original APCs are to be increased and the corresponding cumulative value for ordinary depreciation. The system then posts the increased APC and ordinary depreciation for the selected ledgers and depreciation areas.

Acquisition and Production Costs encompass any asset acquired or purchased externally, including the invoice price and associated expenses such as customs, octroi, and freight. By factoring in these additional costs, you can determine the total acquisition cost essential for the capitalization of the asset.

Learn more about this efficient automation process by exploring the SAP Help API Documentation.

Next, the Asset Accountant wants to validate the revaluation postings with a list of journal entries and understand the depreciation applied to each asset using an explanation of the depreciation key configuration.

SAP Build Process Automation enables the automation of complex scenarios with ease using the recorder. By automatically capturing the "Manage Fixed Asset" application, it allows for the simultaneous design of automations, leveraging enhanced recording features. These features include recording applications combining screens on SAP GUI for Windows and UI automation technologies, enabling a seamless session that includes both Windows and Web (including UI5) interfaces.

Using a UI-based automation bot scheduled at the desired business frequency, the bot navigates to the “Manage Fixed Assets (New)” Fiori App for specific assets with revaluated postings. The bot executes UI-based navigation with a scheduled job to generate and export the list of journal entries for revaluation postings. This list includes details such as Asset Transaction Type, Ledger, Line Item, Posting Date, Valuation Date, and references, which are then emailed to business users including the Asset Accountant.

Additionally, the bot emails the Asset Accountant and defined business users an AI-assisted explanation of the Depreciation Key Configuration. This provides a better understanding of how depreciation keys operate and the rationale behind the system’s complex depreciation calculations for the specific asset.

An AI-powered, user-friendly explanation of depreciation keys facilitates quicker asset onboarding, enhances business user productivity in tracking asset value throughout its lifecycle and revaluation postings, and streamlines periodic activities. This efficiency reduces the time required to understand depreciation and asset values, driving overall operational effectiveness.

AI-assisted Explanation of Depreciation Key Configuration  (J62 - Beta) 

This AI powered feature is available only  BETA Customers & Partners and you can  find more information, on how  Get Involved Early and register @ Influence portal and experience the latest AI features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and influence upcoming AI developments.

In this business use case, the company provides assets, such as laptops and ergonomic office furniture, to consultants and project resources. Asset Accountants face the ongoing challenge of entering newly acquired assets and their inventory numbers and IMEIs into the system before distribution and activation for depreciation.

To address this, the company evaluates and customizes the SAP Build Process Automation Bot template to suit the specific needs of Asset Accountants for mass asset acquisition and creation.

The Asset Accountant uploads a pre-filled template to trigger an approval workflow, sending a notification to the Asset Manager for review. Upon the Asset Manager's approval, the automation posts the acquisition of assets in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system. The Asset Accountant then receives a detailed report.

Mass Fixed Asset Acquisition SAP Build Process Automation Template

Additionally, SAP Build Process Automation template content streamlines the posting of asset acquisitions by fetching existing unposted fixed assets from the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system into a template file, which users update with the necessary asset information. The updated file is then used to extract asset data and utilize the Fixed Asset Acquisition APIs to post the assets. This automation not only saves time and reduces manual errors but also improves overall efficiency. For more details, refer to the documentation on the  SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

As the company continues to acquire new assets, it must create and maintain main and sub-asset numbers on an ongoing basis. To support this, SAP Build Process Automation includes API-driven action tasks to facilitate the creation of main asset number (Learn more about Fixed Asset - Create Main Asset (Asynchronous) with API Documentation, create an sub asset Fixed Asset - Create Sub Asset (Asynchronous) (Learn more with API Documentation)

In specific scenarios, Asset Managers need to update existing assets to reflect changes in master records and valuation information.

SAP Build Process Automation’s API-based Action Task automates these updates, including modifications to the company code, main asset number, asset sub-number, origin information, account assignment, ledger details, valuation data, and time-based valuation information. This streamlined process ensures accuracy and efficiency. For more details, refer to the Fixed Asset - Change (Asynchronous). API documentation .

For more information about the extensibility of the Manage Fixed Assets app, see App Extensibility: Manage Fixed Assets (Documentation)

Visit the SAP Business Accelerator Hub to learn more about 3900+ prebuilt integration scenarios, 1300+ business events, over 250+ connectors to SAP and non-SAP applications, 1200+ automation content, and 35+ integrations all in one place, the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

Stay tuned for more updates in the next quarters!

Follow us via @sap and #S4HANA, or myself via@har1234 (Hardeep Tulsi)

Stay tuned for more updates in the next quarters!

 Where to Find More Information:

  • Watch the replays of our exclusive SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series here: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/watch-the-replays-of-our-exclusive-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-2408/ba-p/13757250
  • Explore the ready-to-run cloud ERP here: https://www.sap.com/products/erp/s4hana.html
  • Watch our SAP S/4HANA Cloud product update videos on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDnnyN2j-CcUheNN-GaNCb3H
  • Check out our digital enablement wheel here: https://chart-bdmaicr0au.dispatcher.eu2.hana.ondemand.com/index.html?hc_reset
  • Learn more about GROW here: https://www.sap.com/products/erp/grow.html
  • Become an early adopter for Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition here: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#campaign/3612
  • Check out the SAP Activate Roadmap for early adopters here: https://go.support.sap.com/roadmapviewer/#/group/658F507A-D6F5-4B78-9EE1-0300C5F1E40F/roadmapOvervie...
  • Review the PPL content here: https://go.support.sap.com/roadmapviewer/#/group/658F507A-D6F5-4B78-9EE1-0300C5F1E40F/roadmapContent...
  • Listen to our podcast here: https://podcast.opensap.info/inside-sap/
  • Watch our openSAP microlearnings to learn about SAP S/4HANA Cloud topics at your own pace here: https://microlearning.opensap.com/
  • Find best practices on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition here: https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-013/latest?region=DE
  • Check out what’s new here: https://help.sap.com/doc/ce01d82756b947a1a043a5d5a3204226  
  • Find help here: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD
  • Read the feature scope description here: https://help.sap.com/doc/7c9e0bbbd1664c2581b2038a1c7ae4b3

entry.title: SAP Build sessions for week 2 of Devtoberfest
entry.content:
